description: A dessert based on a creamy cheese filling set on a crust, often made from crushed biscuits or pastry. Styles vary widely, including baked New York cheesecake, lighter no-bake versions, and regional styles such as Basque cheesecake.
preparation_steps: Prepare the crust and press it into the base of the pan if using one.
Mix the filling until smooth without overworking it.
Bake or chill the cheesecake depending on the style.
Cool fully before slicing and adding toppings.

health_advice.benefits: Provides some calcium and protein from the cheese base.
health_advice.risks: Usually high in calories, sugar, and saturated fat.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Serve small slices and use fruit as a topping instead of relying only on sugary sauces.

dietary_notes.note: Traditional cheesecake usually contains dairy, eggs, and a wheat-based crust. Vegan and gluten-free versions are possible with substitutions.
